name: pubchem-database
description: Query PubChem via PUG-REST API/PubChemPy (110M+ compounds). Search by name/CID/SMILES, retrieve properties, similarity/substructure searches, bioactivity, for cheminformatics.
capabilities: Search 110M+ chemical compounds by name, CID, SMILES, or InChI
Retrieve molecular properties (MW, LogP, TPSA, hydrogen bonding)
Access bioactivity data from screening assays
Download molecular structures as images or SDF files
author: K-Dense-AI
securitySummary: This is a legitimate scientific tool for querying the PubChem chemical database. Static analysis findings are false positives: the 'external commands' are Python code examples in markdown documentation, 'network' calls go to official NIH/NCBI servers, and 'weak cryptographic algorithm' detections are keyword false positives in a non-cryptographic context. No actual cryptographic code, command injection risks, or malicious behavior exists.
valueStatement: Researchers need quick access to molecular properties and chemical structures. This skill connects to PubChem's 110M+ compound database for instant cheminformatics queries.
limitations: Requires internet connection to access PubChem API
Rate limited to 5 requests per second maximum
Some properties unavailable for certain compounds

useCases.title: Drug candidate screening
useCases.description: Screen compounds for drug-like properties using Lipinski's Rule of Five

useCases.title: Structure-activity analysis
useCases.description: Find structurally similar compounds and compare their bioactivities

useCases.title: Chemical identifier conversion
useCases.description: Convert between SMILES, InChI, and compound names for publications

promptTemplates.title: Basic compound search
promptTemplates.prompt: Search PubChem for aspirin and show its molecular formula, weight, and SMILES

promptTemplates.title: Property comparison
promptTemplates.prompt: Get molecular properties for aspirin, ibuprofen, and naproxen. Compare their LogP values

promptTemplates.title: Similarity search
promptTemplates.prompt: Search for compounds similar to imatinib with 85% similarity threshold, then filter by MW 200-600

promptTemplates.title: Substructure screening
promptTemplates.prompt: Find all compounds containing a benzene ring and calculate their average molecular weight

bestPractices: Use CIDs instead of names for repeated queries to improve performance
Implement 0.2-0.3 second delays between requests to respect rate limits
Cache results locally for frequently accessed compounds
antiPatterns: Don't make more than 5 requests per second to avoid rate limits
Avoid searching by common names without checking synonyms first
Don't ignore error handling for compounds that might not exist

faq.question: What is PubChem?
faq.answer: PubChem is the world's largest freely available chemical database with over 110 million compounds, maintained by NCBI/NIH.

faq.question: How do I search by chemical structure?
faq.answer: Use SMILES strings or InChI notation. Example: 'CC(=O)OC1=CC=CC=C1C(=O)O' for aspirin.

faq.question: What properties can I retrieve?
faq.answer: Molecular weight, LogP, TPSA, hydrogen bond counts, and over 50 other molecular descriptors.

faq.question: Why am I getting timeout errors?
faq.answer: Similarity and substructure searches are asynchronous and may take 15-30 seconds. Reduce MaxRecords if needed.

faq.question: Can I download molecular structures?
faq.answer: Yes, you can download as PNG images, SDF files, or JSON format using the download functions.

faq.question: Is there a rate limit?
faq.answer: Yes, maximum 5 requests per second and 400 requests per minute to ensure fair usage.
